sections.content: The Toy Donation Program Feature allows users to donate physical toys to shelter pets through the website. This feature addresses customer requests for material donations and provides an alternative to monetary donations, enabling users to contribute toys they no longer need directly to animals awaiting adoption.

entity_refs.page_title: Green Color for Money-Related UI Elements
entity_refs.item_text: Toy Donation Program Feature implementing green for monetary UI elements

sections.content: This feature builds upon the existing donation infrastructure established in the completed Donation Page project. It follows Kim's established design convention of using green color for money-related UI elements, which must be applied to any financial transaction buttons. The existing donation page provides patterns for payment processing and user experience flows that can be adapted for toy purchases. Customer feedback shows clear demand for toy donation capabilities, with multiple inquiries about donating gently used toys and purchasing toys directly for shelter animals.

sections.content:
- Users must be able to browse available toys for donation/purchase
- Payment processing must be integrated for toy purchases
- Toy selection must allow dedication to specific pets (similar to existing monetary donations)
- All money-related UI elements must use green color per Kim's convention
- Feature must integrate with existing pet browsing and selection patterns
- Confirmation and receipt functionality must be provided
- Feature must handle both purchased new toys and coordination of used toy donations

sections.content:
1. Design toy browsing interface following existing donation page
Create toy catalog page using the same clean design approach as the existing donation page. Apply Kim's green color convention to all purchase/donate buttons. Use neutral gray for toy selection buttons and green for the final 'Purchase Toys' or 'Complete Toy Donation' button, maintaining consistency with the existing 'Complete Donation' button styling.

2. Implement toy-to-pet dedication feature
Adapt the existing 'dedicate to a pet' functionality from the monetary donation page. When users select to dedicate toys to a specific pet, show the pet selector that loads all adoptable pets using the same endpoint as the adoption chooser. Display selected pet cards with the standard pink/blue gender accent system established in the pet profile cards.

3. New work to define: Create toy inventory management
New work to define: Design and implement backend systems for managing toy inventory, including catalog management, stock tracking, and integration with shelter fulfillment processes. This includes defining API contracts for toy CRUD operations and inventory management.

4. Integrate payment processing for toy purchases
Extend the existing credit card payment form from the donation page to handle toy purchases. Maintain the clean white background for the payment section to avoid visual noise during card entry. Ensure the payment flow follows the same UX patterns as monetary donations.

5. New work to define: Implement used toy donation
New work to define: Create workflow for users donating gently used toys, including submission forms, quality guidelines, drop-off coordination, and shelter notification systems. This addresses customer requests for donating existing toys they no longer need.

6. New work to define: Create database schema for
New work to define: Design database tables for toy inventory, donation records, and pet-toy associations. Include migration scripts and ensure proper indexing for toy browsing and reporting functionality.

sections.content:
- Test toy browsing and selection UI across responsive breakpoints
- Verify green color application on all financial action buttons per Kim's convention
- Test pet dedication functionality with the existing pet selector endpoint
- Validate payment processing integration with various card types and amounts
- Test toy-to-pet association and confirmation flows
- Edge case testing: out-of-stock toys, payment failures, invalid pet selections
- Cross-browser testing for payment form security and UX
- Test integration with existing donation receipt/confirmation systems

sections.content:
- Inventory management complexity could impact development timeline
- Used toy donation coordination requires operational processes beyond software
- Payment processing fees will apply to toy purchases, affecting cost structure
- Shelter fulfillment capabilities may limit available toy options
- Quality control for used toy donations presents liability considerations
- Integration complexity with existing donation infrastructure
- Need to maintain design consistency with Kim's established financial UI patterns while creating new toy-specific interfaces
